Now, time complexity:

DSU operations are O(alpha(n)) per find/union. But for each request, we do O(R) checks, each involving two find operations. So per request, O(R * alpha(n)). For R up to 1e3, and Q up to 1e3, total is O(1e6 * alpha(n)), which is acceptable.

Now, implement DSU.

Implement DSU with path compression and union by rank.

Now, code for DSU:

class DSU {

    int[] parent;

    int[] rank;

    public DSU(int n) {

        parent = new int[n];

        for (int i=0; i < n; i++) parent[i] = i;

        rank = new int[n];

    }

    public int find(int x) {

        if (parent[x] != x) parent[x] = find(parent[x]);

        return parent[x];

    }

    public void union(int x, int y) {

        int xr = find(x);

        int yr = find(y);

        if (xr == yr) return;

        if (rank[xr] < rank[yr]) {

            parent[xr] = yr;

        } else {

            parent[yr] = xr;

            if (rank[xr] == rank[yr]) rank[xr]++;

        }

    }

}

private int find(int x, int[] parent) {

    if (parent[x] != x) {

        parent[x] = find(parent[x], parent);

    }

    return parent[x];

}

Similarly for the union:

private void union(int x, int[] parent, int[] rank, int y) {

    int xRoot = find(x, parent);

    int yRoot = find(y, parent);

    if (xRoot == yRoot) return;

    if (rank[xRoot] < rank[yRoot]) {

        parent[xRoot] = yRoot;

    } else {

        parent[yRoot] = xRoot;

        if (rank[xRoot] == rank[yRoot]) {

            rank[xRoot]++;

        }

    }

}

class Solution {

    public boolean[] friendRequests(int n, int[][] restrictions, int[][] requests) {

        int[] parent = new int[n];

        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {

            parent[i] = i;

        }

        int[] rank = new int[n];

        boolean[] result = new boolean[requests.length];

        for (int i = 0; i < requests.length; i++) {

            int u = requests[i][0];

            int v = requests[i][1];

            int rootU = find(parent, u);

            int rootV = find(parent, v);

            if (rootU == rootV) {

                result[i] = true;

            } else {

                boolean valid = true;

                for (int[] restriction : restrictions) {

                    int x = restriction[0];

                    int y = restriction[1];

                    int rootX = find(parent, x);

                    int rootY = find(parent, y);

                    int rootUCheck = find(parent, u);

                    int rootVCheck = find(parent, v);

                    if ((rootUCheck == rootX && rootVCheck == rootY) || (rootUCheck == rootY && rootVCheck == rootX)) {

                        valid = false;

                        break;

                    }

                }

                result[i] = valid;

                if (valid) {

                    union(parent, rank, u, v);

                }

            }

        }

        return result;

    }

    private int find(int[] parent, int x) {

        if (parent[x] != x) {

            parent[x] = find(parent, parent[x]);

        }

        return parent[x];

    }

    private void union(int[] parent, int[] rank, int x, int y) {

        int rootX = find(parent, x);

        int rootY = find(parent, y);

        if (rootX != rootY) {

            if (rank[rootX] > rank[rootY]) {

                parent[rootY] = rootX;

            } else {

                parent[rootX] = rootY;

                if (rank[rootX] == rank[rootY]) {

                    rank[rootY]++;

                }

            }

        }

    }

}

public boolean[] friendRequests(int n, int[][] restrictions, int[][] requests) {

    int[] parent = new int[n];

    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {

        parent[i] = i;

    }

    int[] rank = new int[n];

    boolean[] result = new boolean[requests.length];

    for (int i = 0; i < requests.length; i++) {

        int u = requests[i][0];

        int v = requests[i][1];

        int rootU = find(parent, u);

        int rootV = find(parent, v);

        if (rootU == rootV) {

            result[i] = true;

        } else {

            // Check restrictions

            boolean valid = true;

            // Precompute rootU and rootV

            for (int[] r : restrictions) {

                int x = r[0], y = r[1];

                int xRoot = find(parent, x);

                int yRoot = find(parent, y);

                if ( (xRoot == rootU && yRoot == rootV) || (xRoot == rootV && yRoot == rootU) ) {

                    valid = false;

                    break;

                }

            }

            result[i] = valid;

            if (valid) {

                union(parent, rank, u, v);

            }

        }

    }

    return result;

}

But then, the find and union functions need to be implemented as helper methods.

private int find(int[] parent, int x) {

    if (parent[x] != x) {

        parent[x] = find(parent, parent[x]);

    }

    return parent[x];

}

private void union(int[] parent, int[] rank, int x, int y) {

    int rootX = find(parent, x);

    int rootY = find(parent, y);

    if (rootX != rootY) {

        if (rank[rootX] > rank[rootY]) {

            parent[rootY] = rootX;

        } else {

            parent[rootX] = rootY;

            if (rank[rootX] == rank[rootY]) {

                rank[rootY]++;

            }

        }

    }

}

class Solution {

    private int find(int[] parent, int x) {

        if (parent[x] != x) {

            parent[x] = find(parent, parent[x]);

        }

        return parent[x];

    }

    private void union(int[] parent, int[] rank, int x, int y) {

        int rootX = find(parent, x);

        int rootY = find(parent, y);

        if (rootX != rootY) {

            if (rank[rootX] > rank[rootY]) {

                parent[rootY] = rootX;

            } else {

                parent[rootX] = rootY;

                if (rank[rootX] == rank[rootY]) {

                    rank[rootY]++;

                }

            }

        }

    }

    public boolean[] friendRequests(int n, int[][] restrictions, int[][] requests) {

        int[] parent = new int[n];

        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {

            parent[i] = i;

        }

        int[] rank = new int[n];

        boolean[] result = new boolean[requests.length];

        for (int i = 0; i < requests.length; i++) {

            int u = requests[i][0];

            int v = requests[i][1];

            int rootU = find(parent, u);

            int rootV = find(parent, v);

            if (rootU == rootV) {

                result[i] = true;

            } else {

                boolean valid = true;

                for (int[] r : restrictions) {

                    int x = r[0], y = r[1];

                    int xRoot = find(parent, x);

                    int yRoot = find(parent, y);

                    if ((xRoot == rootU && yRoot == rootV) || (xRoot == rootV && yRoot == rootU)) {

                        valid = false;

                        break;

                    }

                }

                result[i] = valid;

                if (valid) {

                    union(parent, rank, u, v);

                }

            }

        }

        return result;

    }

}

Implement DSU with path compression and union by rank.

Now, code for DSU:

class DSU {

    int[] parent;

    int[] rank;

    public DSU(int n) {

        parent = new int[n];

        for (int i=0; i < n; i++) parent[i] = i;

        rank = new int[n];

    }

    public int find(int x) {

        if (parent[x] != x) parent[x] = find(parent[x]);

        return parent[x];

    }

    public void union(int x, int y) {

        int xr = find(x);

        int yr = find(y);

        if (xr == yr) return;

        if (rank[xr] < rank[yr]) {

            parent[xr] = yr;

        } else {

            parent[yr] = xr;

            if (rank[xr] == rank[yr]) rank[xr]++;

        }

    }

}

class Solution {

    public boolean[] friendRequests(int n, int[][] restrictions, int[][] requests) {

        int[] parent = new int[n];

        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {

            parent[i] = i;

        }

        int[] rank = new int[n];

        boolean[] result = new boolean[requests.length];

        for (int i = 0; i < requests.length; i++) {

            int u = requests[i][0];

            int v = requests[i][1];

            int rootU = find(parent, u);

            int rootV = find(parent, v);

            if (rootU == rootV) {

                result[i] = true;

            } else {

                boolean valid = true;

                for (int[] restriction : restrictions) {

                    int x = restriction[0];

                    int y = restriction[1];

                    int rootX = find(parent, x);

                    int rootY = find(parent, y);

                    int rootUCheck = find(parent, u);

                    int rootVCheck = find(parent, v);

                    if ((rootUCheck == rootX && rootVCheck == rootY) || (rootUCheck == rootY && rootVCheck == rootX)) {

                        valid = false;

                        break;

                    }

                }

                result[i] = valid;

                if (valid) {

                    union(parent, rank, u, v);

                }

            }

        }

        return result;

    }

    private int find(int[] parent, int x) {

        if (parent[x] != x) {

            parent[x] = find(parent, parent[x]);

        }

        return parent[x];

    }

    private void union(int[] parent, int[] rank, int x, int y) {

        int rootX = find(parent, x);

        int rootY = find(parent, y);

        if (rootX != rootY) {

            if (rank[rootX] > rank[rootY]) {

                parent[rootY] = rootX;

            } else {

                parent[rootX] = rootY;

                if (rank[rootX] == rank[rootY]) {

                    rank[rootY]++;

                }

            }

        }

    }

}

But why in the previous code example I had code that called find(u) and find(v) again for each restriction? Probably a mistake. So correcting that.

So, in the code, during the check phase, we can precompute rootU and rootV once, then for each restriction, check if x and y are in different components, whose merge would connect them.

This would reduce the number of find operations.

So, this is better. So the code should be modified accordingly.

So, in the code inside the else block:

int rootU = find(parent, u);

int rootV = find(parent, v);

then for each restriction, check if (find(x) is rootU and find(y) is rootV) or vice versa.

This would be better.

So, in the code:

for (int j=0; j < requests.length; j++) {

    ... 

    else {

        int rootU = find(...);

        int rootV = find(...);

        for (each restriction) {

            ... 

        }

    }

}

This would reduce the number of find operations.

So, in the code, during the check phase, for each restriction, we find x and y's roots, and compare to rootU and rootV.

This is better.

So, in the code, the code inside the else block would be:

int rootU = find(parent, u);

int rootV = find(parent, v);

boolean valid = true;

for (int[] r : restrictions) {

    int x = r[0], y = r[1];

    int xRoot = find(parent, x);

    int yRoot = find(parent, y);

    if ( (xRoot == rootU && yRoot == rootV) || (xRoot == rootV && yRoot == rootU) ) {

        valid = false;

        break;

    }

}

res[j] = valid;

if (valid) {

    union(u, v);

}

This is better.

So, this reduces the number of find operations per restriction check from 4 to 2.

This is better for efficiency.

Now, let's code this in Java.

Now, considering the code:

The code for the friendRequests method would be:

public boolean[] friendRequests(int n, int[][] restrictions, int[][] requests) {

    int[] parent = new int[n];

    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {

        parent[i] = i;

    }

    int[] rank = new int[n];

    boolean[] result = new boolean[requests.length];

    for (int i = 0; i < requests.length; i++) {

        int u = requests[i][0];

        int v = requests[i][1];

        int rootU = find(parent, u);

        int rootV = find(parent, v);

        if (rootU == rootV) {

            result[i] = true;

        } else {

            // Check restrictions

            boolean valid = true;

            // Precompute rootU and rootV

            for (int[] r : restrictions) {

                int x = r[0], y = r[1];

                int xRoot = find(parent, x);

                int yRoot = find(parent, y);

                if ( (xRoot == rootU && yRoot == rootV) || (xRoot == rootV && yRoot == rootU) ) {

                    valid = false;

                    break;

                }

            }

            result[i] = valid;

            if (valid) {

                union(parent, rank, u, v);

            }

        }

    }

    return result;

}

But then, the find and union functions need to be implemented as helper methods.

So, adding the helper methods:

private int find(int[] parent, int x) {

    if (parent[x] != x) {

        parent[x] = find(parent, parent[x]);

    }

    return parent[x];

}

private void union(int[] parent, int[] rank, int x, int y) {

    int rootX = find(parent, x);

    int rootY = find(parent, y);

    if (rootX != rootY) {

        if (rank[rootX] > rank[rootY]) {

            parent[rootY] = rootX;

        } else {

            parent[rootX] = rootY;

            if (rank[rootX] == rank[rootY]) {

                rank[rootY]++;

            }

        }

    }

}

But wait, in Java, these helper methods need to be inside the Solution class. Since the starter code provides the Solution class with the friendRequests method, adding these helper methods is allowed.

So, putting it all together, the code would be:

class Solution {

    private int find(int[] parent, int x) {

        if (parent[x] != x) {

            parent[x] = find(parent, parent[x]);

        }

        return parent[x];

    }

    private void union(int[] parent, int[] rank, int x, int y) {

        int rootX = find(parent, x);

        int rootY = find(parent, y);

        if (rootX != rootY) {

            if (rank[rootX] > rank[rootY]) {

                parent[rootY] = rootX;

            } else {

                parent[rootX] = rootY;

                if (rank[rootX] == rank[rootY]) {

                    rank[rootY]++;

                }

            }

        }

    }

    public boolean[] friendRequests(int n, int[][] restrictions, int[][] requests) {

        int[] parent = new int[n];

        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {

            parent[i] = i;

        }

        int[] rank = new int[n];

        boolean[] result = new boolean[requests.length];

        for (int i = 0; i < requests.length; i++) {

            int u = requests[i][0];

            int v = requests[i][1];

            int rootU = find(parent, u);

            int rootV = find(parent, v);

            if (rootU == rootV) {

                result[i] = true;

            } else {

                boolean valid = true;

                for (int[] r : restrictions) {

                    int x = r[0], y = r[1];

                    int xRoot = find(parent, x);

                    int yRoot = find(parent, y);

                    if ((xRoot == rootU && yRoot == rootV) || (xRoot == rootV && yRoot == rootU)) {

                        valid = false;

                        break;

                    }

                }

                result[i] = valid;

                if (valid) {

                    union(parent, rank, u, v);

                }

            }

        }

        return result;

    }

}

Now, testing this code against the examples.

Let's take Example 3:

Input: n=5, restrictions = [[0,1],[1,2],[2,3]], requests = [[0,4],[1,2],[3,1],[3,4]]

Output: [true, false, true, false]

Let's walk through the code.

Initially, parent is [0,1,2,3,4], rank all 0.

First request: [0,4]

rootU is 0, rootV is 4. Not same. Check restrictions:

For each restriction:

First restriction [0,1]. xRoot is 0, yRoot is 1. rootU is 0, rootV is 4. So xRoot is 0 == rootU, yRoot is 1 != rootV (4). So no.

Second restriction [1,2]. xRoot 1, yRoot 2. Not matching 0 and 4.

Third restriction [2,3]. same. So valid is true. So perform union(0,4). Now parent[4] = 0. rank[0] becomes 1.

Result[0] is true.

Second request: [1,2]. rootU is 1, rootV is 2. Not same. Check restrictions:

For each restriction:

First restriction [0,1]. xRoot 0, yRoot 1. Not related to 1 and 2.

Second restriction [1,2]. xRoot is 1, yRoot is 2. So (xRoot == rootU (1) and yRoot == rootV (2). So condition is true. valid becomes false. So result is false. No union.

Third request: [3,1]. rootU is 3, rootV is 1. Not same. Check restrictions:

Check each restriction:

First restriction [0,1]. xRoot 0 (since 0 is root of 1?), no. Let's think:

Current DSU state:

After first request, 0 and 4 are connected. Others are separate.

For the third request, which is [3,1]. root(3) is 3. root(1) is 1. Check restrictions:

For each restriction:

First restriction [0,1]. xRoot is 0, yRoot is 1. rootU (3) and rootV (1). Not matching.

Second restriction [1,2]. xRoot 1, yRoot 2. Not matching 3 and 1.

Third restriction [2,3]. xRoot 2, yRoot 3. rootU is 3, rootV is 1. So check if (xRoot == 3 and yRoot == 1) or (xRoot == 1 and yRoot == 3). For this restriction, x is 2, y is 3. xRoot is 2, yRoot is 3. So no. So no match. So valid is true. So perform union(3,1). Now, parent[3] is 3's root (3) and 1's root (1). So when we do union(3,1), since their roots are different. The rank of 3 and 1 are 0. So parent[3's root] becomes 1's root? Or depends on rank. Since rank[3] is 0, rank[1] is 0. So parent[3's root (3)] is set to 1's root (1). Then, rank[1] increases by 1. Now, the root of 3 is 1. So after this, 3 and 1 are connected.

Result[2] is true.

Fourth request: [3,4]. u is 3, v is 4
